page.title: Sundowner Tapas Cruise from Puerto Banús

data.meta_description: Book a sundowner tapas cruise Marbella from Puerto Banús. Enjoy tapas, drinks, and sunset views from €749 for 2 hours on a luxury boat. Book now.
data.summary: This sundowner tapas cruise Marbella departs Puerto Banús, offering tapas, drinks, and sunset views on luxury boats like the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, and snacks.
key_facts: Prices from €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and light tapas-style snacks
Available on Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax)
Spanish IVA (21%) included in all prices
No hidden extras – fuel, insurance, and crew included

faq.q: What is included in a sundowner tapas cruise Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light tapas-style snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just need to bring your party.

faq.q: Which boats are available for the sundowner tapas cruise?
faq.a: The sundowner tapas cruise is available on our Tier A boats: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 guests) and the Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 guests). Both depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How much does a sundowner tapas cruise from Puerto Banús cost?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. For longer or custom durations, contact us via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What time does the sundowner cruise usually depart?
faq.a: The cruise typically departs around 19:00 to catch the sunset, but times vary by season. We recommend booking in advance to secure your preferred slot.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.a: Our cruises already include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light tapas snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or drinks if you wish, but it’s not necessary.

faq.q: Is the sundowner tapas cruise suitable for large groups?
faq.a: Yes. The Astondoa 40 accommodates up to 9 guests, and the Azimut 39 up to 11 guests. For larger parties, consider our larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (up to 12 guests) with a 4-hour minimum.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety is our priority. If weather conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule your cruise or provide a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely.

faq.q: How do I book a sundowner tapas cruise Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom requests. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak season.

data.body_html:
There’s no better way to end a Marbella day than a sundowner tapas cruise from Puerto Banús. As the sun sinks behind La Concha, you’ll glide past the Golden Mile with a chilled glass of cava and a plate of olives, all while the Mediterranean turns to gold. Our sundowner tapas cruise Marbella packages start at just €749 for two hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and light tapas snacks — no hidden extras.

Why choose a sundowner tapas cruise from Puerto Banús?

Puerto Banús is the perfect departure point for a sunset sail. You’ll cruise past the marina’s superyachts, then head towards Cabopino or Cala del Faro. The light at dusk is spectacular, and the sea breeze keeps things cool even in August. Our sundowner tapas cruise Marbella is designed for relaxation — no rushing, no crowds, just you and your group.

Which boats are available for the sundowner tapas cruise?

We offer two Tier A boats ideal for this experience:

  • Astondoa 40 – 12.5m, up to 9 guests, €749/2h to €2,299/8h
  • Azimut 39 – 12.5m, up to 11 guests, €749/2h to €2,299/8h

Both depart from Puerto Banús and come fully crewed with a licensed skipper. For larger groups (up to 12 guests), consider our Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 (from €4,719 for 4 hours, jet ski included). See our full boat rental Marbella fleet for more options.

What’s included in the sundowner tapas cruise Marbella?

Every charter includes:

  • Licensed skipper
  • Fuel
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light tapas snacks (olives, nuts, cheese, cured meats, bread)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

There are no hidden costs. You can bring extra food or drinks if you wish, but we provide everything you need for a perfect sunset evening.

How much does a sundowner tapas cruise cost?

BoatDurationPrice (all inclusive)
Astondoa 402 hours€749
Astondoa 408 hours€2,299
Azimut 392 hours€749
Azimut 398 hours€2,299

For longer durations or custom requests, contact us via WhatsApp for pricing.

Best sunset spots on the Marbella coast

Your skipper knows the best anchorages for sunset. Popular stops include:

  • Cala del Faro – a quiet cove with views of the lighthouse
  • Cabopino – sandy beach and calm waters
  • Estepona coast – longer cruise with mountain backdrop
  • Sotogrande – exclusive area with luxury villas

You can also stay close to Puerto Banús and watch the marina lights come on as the sky darkens.

What to expect on board

Your skipper will welcome you at Puerto Banús, help you board, and brief you on safety. Once underway, you’ll enjoy drinks and tapas at your leisure. There’s no fixed itinerary — you can relax on deck, take photos, or even swim if conditions allow. The whole experience is private, so it’s just your group and the crew.

How to book your sundowner tapas cruise Marbella

Booking is simple. Choose your boat and duration, then contact us via WhatsApp or use our online booking system. We recommend reserving at least 48 hours in advance, especially in July and August. For last-minute bookings, call us directly.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a sundowner tapas cruise Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light tapas-style snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just need to bring your party.

Which boats are available for the sundowner tapas cruise?

The sundowner tapas cruise is available on our Tier A boats: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 guests) and the Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 guests). Both depart from Puerto Banús.

How much does a sundowner tapas cruise from Puerto Banús cost?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT. For longer or custom durations, contact us via WhatsApp.

What time does the sundowner cruise usually depart?

The cruise typically departs around 19:00 to catch the sunset, but times vary by season. We recommend booking in advance to secure your preferred slot.

Can I bring my own food or drinks?

Our cruises already include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light tapas snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or drinks if you wish, but it’s not necessary.

Is the sundowner tapas cruise suitable for large groups?

Yes. The Astondoa 40 accommodates up to 9 guests, and the Azimut 39 up to 11 guests. For larger parties, consider our larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (up to 12 guests) with a 4-hour minimum.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety is our priority. If weather conditions are unsafe, we will reschedule your cruise or provide a full refund. We monitor forecasts closely.

How do I book a sundowner tapas cruise Marbella?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom requests. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak season.
